Version
  • Submodules
  • Hosting
  • Hosting

Hosting

The AgentFramework.Hosting submodule provides a local hosted runtime for running agents and workflows over HTTP. It bundles session persistence, conversation history, workflow checkpointing, and a REST API server into a single HostedRuntime object.

Overview

HostedRuntime is a self-contained server that:

  • Registers agents and workflows by name
  • Manages sessions with automatic history tracking
  • Runs durable workflows with checkpointing and human-in-the-loop resume
  • Exposes a REST API via an HTTP server built on HTTP.jl

Quick Start

using AgentFramework
using AgentFramework.Hosting

# Create a runtime (in-memory by default)
runtime = HostedRuntime()

# Or persist to disk
runtime = HostedRuntime(storage_dir)

# Register an agent
agent = Agent(
    name = "helper",
    instructions = "You are a helpful assistant.",
    client = OpenAIChatClient(model = "gpt-4o-mini"),
)
register_agent!(runtime, agent)

# Run via Julia API
result = run_agent!(runtime, "helper", "Hello!")
println(get_text(result.response))
println("Session: ", result.session.id)

# Or start an HTTP server
serve(runtime; host = "127.0.0.1", port = 8080)
Docs example response for: Hello!
Session: da4245b0-e3ad-43ee-975f-3dd5a15e8122

HostedRuntime

Base.@kwdef mutable struct HostedRuntime
    agents::Dict{String, Agent}
    workflows::Dict{String, Workflow}
    session_store::AbstractSessionStore
    history_provider::AbstractHistoryProvider
    checkpoint_storage::AbstractCheckpointStorage
    run_store::AbstractHostedRunStore
    lock::ReentrantLock
end

Constructors

In-memory runtime (no persistence):

runtime = HostedRuntime()
AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedRuntime(Dict{String, Agent}(), Dict{String, Workflow}(), InMemorySessionStore(Dict{String, AgentSession}(), ReentrantLock()), InMemoryHistoryProvider(0 sessions), InMemoryCheckpointStorage(Dict{String, WorkflowCheckpoint}(), ReentrantLock()), AgentFramework.Hosting.InMemoryHostedRunStore(Dict{String, AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedWorkflowRun}(), ReentrantLock()), ReentrantLock())

File-backed runtime (persists sessions, history, checkpoints, and workflow runs to disk):

runtime = HostedRuntime(storage_dir)
AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedRuntime(Dict{String, Agent}(), Dict{String, Workflow}(), FileSessionStore("/var/folders/yh/30rj513j6mn1n7x556c2v4w80000gn/T/jl_4SwGYa/storage/sessions", ReentrantLock()), FileHistoryProvider("/var/folders/yh/30rj513j6mn1n7x556c2v4w80000gn/T/jl_4SwGYa/storage/history"), FileCheckpointStorage("/var/folders/yh/30rj513j6mn1n7x556c2v4w80000gn/T/jl_4SwGYa/storage/checkpoints"), AgentFramework.Hosting.FileHostedRunStore("/var/folders/yh/30rj513j6mn1n7x556c2v4w80000gn/T/jl_4SwGYa/storage/runs", ReentrantLock()), ReentrantLock())

The directory-based constructor creates subdirectories for sessions/, history/, checkpoints/, and runs/.

Agent Management

register_agent!

Register an Agent with the runtime under a given name.

register_agent!(runtime, agent)                    # uses agent.name
register_agent!(runtime, agent; name = "my-agent")  # custom name
Agent("helper", 0 tools)

Throws ArgumentError if an agent with that name is already registered.

list_registered_agents

names = list_registered_agents(runtime)  # => ["helper", "reviewer"]
2-element Vector{String}:
 "helper"
 "my-agent"

run_agent!

Run a registered agent with input, automatically managing session lifecycle.

result = run_agent!(runtime, "helper", "What is 2+2?")
result = run_agent!(runtime, "helper", "Follow up"; session_id = result.session.id)
(response = AgentResponse(Message[Message(:assistant, "Docs example response for: Follow up")], nothing, nothing, STOP, nothing, "docs-example-model", Dict{String, Any}(), nothing, nothing), session = AgentSession("ff322df9-c177-4f18-bd53-4c790f578590"), history = Message[Message(:user, "What is 2+2?"), Message(:assistant, "Docs example response for: What is 2+2?"), Message(:user, "Follow up"), Message(:assistant, "Docs example response for: Follow up")])

Returns a named tuple (response, session, history):

  • response — the AgentResponse
  • session — the AgentSession (created or loaded)
  • history — Vector{Message} of the conversation so far

get_agent_session

Retrieve an existing session and its conversation history.

info = get_agent_session(runtime, "helper", result.session.id)
info.session   # => AgentSession
info.history   # => Vector{Message}
4-element Vector{Message}:
 Message(:user, "What is 2+2?")
 Message(:assistant, "Docs example response for: What is 2+2?")
 Message(:user, "Follow up")
 Message(:assistant, "Docs example response for: Follow up")

list_agent_sessions

sessions = list_agent_sessions(runtime, "helper")
2-element Vector{AgentSession}:
 AgentSession("da4245b0-e3ad-43ee-975f-3dd5a15e8122")
 AgentSession("ff322df9-c177-4f18-bd53-4c790f578590")

delete_agent_session!

deleted = delete_agent_session!(runtime, "helper", session_id)  # => true/false
false

Workflow Management

register_workflow!

Register a Workflow with the runtime.

register_workflow!(runtime, workflow)
register_workflow!(runtime, workflow; name = "my-workflow")
Workflow("docs-workflow", 2 executors, 1 edges)

list_registered_workflows

names = list_registered_workflows(runtime)
2-element Vector{String}:
 "docs-workflow"
 "my-workflow"

start_workflow_run!

Start a new workflow run. The workflow is deep-copied so the blueprint is not modified.

run = start_workflow_run!(runtime, "my-workflow", input_data)
run = start_workflow_run!(runtime, "my-workflow", input_data; run_id = "custom-id", metadata = Dict("key" => "value"))
AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedWorkflowRun("custom-id", "my-workflow", "my-workflow", WF_STARTED, nothing, Any[], Dict{String, Any}[], Dict{String, Any}[], nothing, Dates.DateTime("2026-04-18T00:55:39.289"), Dates.DateTime("2026-04-18T00:55:39.289"), Dict{String, Any}("key" => "value"))

Returns a HostedWorkflowRun.

get_workflow_run

run = get_workflow_run(runtime, "my-workflow", run.id)
AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedWorkflowRun("custom-id", "my-workflow", "my-workflow", WF_STARTED, nothing, Any[], Dict{String, Any}[], Dict{String, Any}[], nothing, Dates.DateTime("2026-04-18T00:55:39.398"), Dates.DateTime("2026-04-18T00:55:39.398"), Dict{String, Any}())

list_workflow_runs

runs = list_workflow_runs(runtime, "my-workflow")
runs = list_workflow_runs(runtime)  # all workflows
1-element Vector{AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedWorkflowRun}:
 AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedWorkflowRun("docs-run-id", "docs-workflow", "docs-workflow", WF_STARTED, nothing, Any[], Dict{String, Any}[], Dict{String, Any}[], nothing, Dates.DateTime("2026-04-18T00:55:39.413"), Dates.DateTime("2026-04-18T00:55:39.413"), Dict{String, Any}())

resume_workflow_run!

Resume a paused workflow (e.g., after human-in-the-loop approval).

run = resume_workflow_run!(runtime, "my-workflow", run.id, Dict("request_id" => "approved"))
AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedWorkflowRun("custom-id", "my-workflow", "my-workflow", WF_STARTED, nothing, Any[], Dict{String, Any}[], Dict{String, Any}[], nothing, Dates.DateTime("2026-04-18T00:55:39.542"), Dates.DateTime("2026-04-18T00:55:39.542"), Dict{String, Any}("request_id" => "approved"))

HostedWorkflowRun

Base.@kwdef mutable struct HostedWorkflowRun
    id::String
    workflow_name::String
    internal_workflow_name::String
    state::WorkflowRunState
    checkpoint_id::Union{Nothing, String}
    outputs::Vector{Any}
    pending_requests::Vector{Dict{String, Any}}
    events::Vector{Dict{String, Any}}
    error::Union{Nothing, String}
    created_at::DateTime
    updated_at::DateTime
    metadata::Dict{String, Any}
end

Use hosted_workflow_run_to_dict(run) to serialize a run to a JSON-compatible dictionary.

Run Stores

The runtime uses an AbstractHostedRunStore to persist workflow runs. Two implementations are provided:

InMemoryHostedRunStore

Stores runs in a thread-safe in-memory dictionary. Data is lost when the process exits.

store = InMemoryHostedRunStore()
AgentFramework.Hosting.InMemoryHostedRunStore(Dict{String, AgentFramework.Hosting.HostedWorkflowRun}(), ReentrantLock())

FileHostedRunStore

Stores each run as a JSON file in a directory.

store = FileHostedRunStore(runs_dir)
AgentFramework.Hosting.FileHostedRunStore("/var/folders/yh/30rj513j6mn1n7x556c2v4w80000gn/T/jl_4SwGYa/runs", ReentrantLock())

Both stores implement:

  • load_run(store, run_id) — load a run (returns nothing if not found)
  • save_run!(store, run) — persist a run
  • delete_run!(store, run_id) — delete a run
  • list_runs(store) / list_runs(store, workflow_name) — list runs

HTTP Server & REST API

serve

Start an HTTP server backed by the runtime.

serve(runtime; host = "127.0.0.1", port = 8080)

handle_request

Process a single HTTP.Request and return an HTTP.Response. Useful for testing or embedding in a custom server.

response = handle_request(runtime, request)
HTTP.Messages.Response:
"""
HTTP/1.1 200 OK

{"status":"ok"}"""

API Endpoints

MethodPathDescription
GET/healthHealth check ({"status": "ok"})
GET/agentsList registered agents
GET/workflowsList registered workflows
POST/agents/{name}/runRun an agent ({"message": "...", "session_id": "...", "options": {...}})
GET/agents/{name}/sessionsList sessions for an agent
GET/agents/{name}/sessions/{id}Get session details and history
DELETE/agents/{name}/sessions/{id}Delete a session
POST/workflows/{name}/runsStart a workflow run ({"input": ..., "run_id": "...", "metadata": {...}})
GET/workflows/{name}/runsList workflow runs
GET/workflows/{name}/runs/{id}Get a specific workflow run
POST/workflows/{name}/runs/{id}/resumeResume a paused run ({"responses": {...}})

Example: Calling the API with curl

# Health check
curl http://localhost:8080/health

# Run an agent
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/agents/helper/run \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"message": "What is Julia?"}'

# Continue a conversation
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/agents/helper/run \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"message": "Tell me more", "session_id": "abc-123"}'

# Start a workflow
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/workflows/my-workflow/runs \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"input": "some data"}'
« A2A ProtocolMem0 Integration »
